_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-national autopilot)
_meta.note: VA federal U.S. House batch (federal tier, 50 cells). Two incumbents w/ real records (Jennifer McClellan D VA-04; John McGuire R VA-05) + three VA-07/VA-02 challengers scored via social-media/self-statement protocol (Patrick Mosolf D VA-02; Darius Mayfield R VA-07; Douglas Ollivant R VA-07). CANDIDACY CURRENCY: the VA congressional primary is AUGUST 4, 2026 (not June) — all three challengers verified STILL ACTIVE/pre-primary via Ballotpedia + active FEC committees. McGuire: Heritage 100 / SBA A+ / NRA-endorsed, uniformly conservative record. McClellan: uniformly progressive (NARAL/HRC endorsed, AWB cosponsor, anti-Laken-Riley). DEVIATION FLAGS: Ollivant's establishment-hawk 'peace through strength' posture cuts AGAINST the foreign-policy-restraint rubric (noted, not scored from a slogan); McGuire's TPAction-flagged FISA/Sec702 surveillance YES vote + lone 2024 state vote against raising marriage age (noted, no clean federal cell). Challenger records are THIN by design — only clearly self-stated positions scored; all else null (never inferred from party).

profile.background: U.S. Representative, VA-04, since the Feb 2023 special election (succeeded the late Donald McEachin); first Black woman elected to Congress from Virginia. Former VA state senator. Seeking reelection 2026 (Democratic primary; she announced May 2026).

jennifer-mcclellan.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-04 (D), incumbent seeking reelection 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — evidence-first.] Uniformly progressive HARD record. LIFE: endorsed by NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All; original cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act + EACH Act (repeal Hyde); voted against House pro-life bills. MARRIAGE/SEX: endorsed by Human Rights Campaign; member of the Congressional Equality Caucus; cosponsor of the Transgender Bill of Rights; voted NO on H.R.28 (Protection of Women & Girls in Sports, Roll Call 12, 1/14/2025). ELECTIONS: cosponsor of the John Lewis VRAA + Freedom to Vote Act (expand access) — opposite of photo-ID/single-day/anti-mail-in positions. BORDER: voted NO on the Laken Riley Act (S.5) on due-process grounds. GUNS: cosponsor of the Assault Weapons Ban of 2025 (H.R.3115). Heritage Action lifetime ~3-6%. No cross-party (rightward) deviation found. NOTE: her CBDC (H.R.1919) and 118th-Congress HR2 individual roll calls were NOT confirmed from a primary source — left null, not inferred. Her NO vote on the Israel supplemental (H.R.4821) was to her LEFT (wanted Ukraine attached / pro-Palestinian), not a restraint position — foreign-policy-restraint left null.

0.note: Endorsed by NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All; original cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Cosponsor of the WHPA + EACH Act (repeal of the Hyde Amendment) — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Accepted the NARAL Pro-Choice America endorsement (abortion-industry advocacy group).

0.note: HRC-endorsed and a member of the Congressional Equality Caucus; does not affirm marriage as one man + one woman.

1.note: Pro-LGBTQ record (HRC endorsement, Equality Caucus) — supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Voted NO on H.R.28 Protection of Women & Girls in Sports (1/14/2025); cosponsor of the Transgender Bill of Rights — rejects biological-sex framing.

4.note: Equality Caucus member; opposed the women's-sports bills and cosponsors LGBTQ-promotion measures.

1.note: Cosponsor of the John Lewis VRAA + Freedom to Vote Act — opposes photo-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions.

2.note: Backs expanded voting access, opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Freedom to Vote Act expands mail/early voting — opposite of opposing mass mail-in voting.

1.note: Voted NO on the Laken Riley Act (S.5), objecting to mandatory detention/deportation on due-process grounds.
Cosponsor of the Assault Weapons Ban of 2025 (H.R.3115) — supports an 'assault weapons' ban.

profile.background: U.S. Representative, VA-05, since Jan 2025 (beat incumbent Bob Good in the 2024 GOP primary). Former Navy SEAL; former VA Delegate (2018-2024) and VA Senator (2024-25). Seeking reelection 2026 (GOP primary Aug 4, 2026).

john-mcguire.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-05 (R), incumbent seeking reelection 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — evidence-first.] Uniformly conservative HARD record: Heritage Action 100% (session + lifetime), SBA Pro-Life A+ (119th), NRA-endorsed, Turning Point Action ~86 overall. LIFE: voted for + cosponsored the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act; SBA A+. SEX/SPORTS: voted for + cosponsored H.R.28 (Protection of Women & Girls in Sports); sponsored the Riley Gaines Act. FAMILY: TPAction Educational Freedom 100%; voted Yes on the PROTECT Kids Act (parental notification on gender). CBDC: cosponsor of the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (H.R.1919). ELECTIONS: cosponsored + voted for the SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship to register). BORDER: voted for + cosponsored the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29). 2A: NRA-endorsed, sponsored the 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act of 2026' + Veterans 2A Protection Act. HEALTH FREEDOM: opposed COVID vaccine mandates for the National Guard; TPAction Health & Medical Freedom 100%. FLAGGED (noted, not scored — no clean federal cell): (1) TPAction flags a FISA/Section-702 surveillance YES vote AGAINST their position (a civil-liberties deviation); (2) as a VA Senator in 2024 he cast the lone vote against raising the legal marriage age 16->18 ('we need more families') — drew bipartisan criticism.

0.note: SBA Pro-Life America A+ (119th); voted for + cosponsored the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act — affirms the pro-life position.

4.note: SBA Pro-Life A+ and pro-life-group endorsed; no abortion-industry money found.

2.note: Voted for + cosponsored H.R.28 (Protection of Women & Girls in Sports); sponsored the Riley Gaines Act — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Sponsored the Riley Gaines Act opposing transgender athletes in women's sports — opposes LGBTQ-ideology promotion in policy.

0.note: Turning Point Action 'Educational Freedom' 100% — supports school-choice/educational-freedom legislation.

1.note: Voted Yes on the PROTECT Kids Act (parental notification when a school changes a child's gender markers).

0.note: Cosponsor of the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (H.R.1919) — opposes a Central Bank Digital Currency.

1.note: Cosponsored + voted for the SAVE Act (documentary proof of citizenship to register) — supports voter-ID/citizenship verification.
Voted for + cosponsored the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29) — mandatory detention/deportation of criminal illegal aliens.
NRA-endorsed; sponsored the 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act of 2026' + Veterans 2A Protection Act — opposes gun-control infringements.

4.note: TPAction 2nd Amendment 100%; sponsored anti-gun-free-zone legislation — opposes citizen-disarmament initiatives.

0.note: Opposed COVID vaccine mandates for the National Guard ('scientifically unfounded, illegitimate'); TPAction Health & Medical Freedom 100% — opposes vaccine mandates.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-02 (2026 D Candidate · challenger · Kiggans challenger)

profile.background: 2026 Democratic challenger for U.S. House VA-02 (challenging Rep. Jen Kiggans, R). Background in international democracy/anti-corruption work. Active FEC committee 'Mosolf for Congress' (H6VA02164). Democratic primary Aug 4, 2026 (verified pre-primary, not withdrawn).

patrick-mosolf.notes_set: 2026 Democratic challenger, VA-02 (Kiggans challenger). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — social-media/self-statement protocol.] CANDIDACY CURRENCY VERIFIED: still an active declared candidate, NOT withdrawn — active FEC committee (H6VA02164, Statement of Candidacy 8/16/2025; reported through 3/31/2026) and a live campaign site; Democratic primary is AUGUST 4, 2026 (corrects an earlier 'June' assumption). Self-stated positions (campaign Issues/About page) are confined to: extending ACA subsidies + a public-plan buy-in, a minimum-wage increase, a corporate-tax restructure for wealth redistribution, 'a more rational tariff policy,' electoral-college criticism, AI-control treaties, and pharmaceutical-price regulation. NONE of these map to a specific RESOLUTE rubric cell. On every one of the scorecard's moral/cultural questions — abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, school choice, religious liberty, guns, immigration, vaccines, foreign wars — he has published NO citable position, so all cells are null (not inferred from party). No cross-party deviation found. Thin record by design: he is scored on what he has actually said, which is off-rubric.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (2026 R Candidate · 2x prior NJ-12 nominee · talent manager)

profile.background: 2026 Republican candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (seat held by Rep. Eugene Vindman, D); talent manager; 2x prior GOP nominee for NJ-12. Active FEC committee 'Mayfield for Congress' (H6VA07171). Contested GOP primary Aug 4, 2026 (verified active, not withdrawn).

darius-mayfield.notes_set: 2026 Republican candidate, VA-07 (one of five in the GOP primary). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — social-media/self-statement protocol.] CANDIDACY CURRENCY VERIFIED: active declared candidate, NOT withdrawn — active FEC committee (H6VA07171, filed 4/4/2025) + Ballotpedia listing; contested GOP primary AUGUST 4, 2026 (corrects an earlier 'June' assumption). Scored only from clear self-statements: current campaign platform (dariusmayfield.com) HARD positions on completing the border wall + immigration enforcement (border q0 TRUE) and a school-choice advocacy foundation he runs (family q0 TRUE); documented pro-life worldview (argued abortion is not a constitutional right, per Ballotpedia-reported podcast remarks; iSideWith lists 'Pro-life' — sanctity q0 TRUE). THIN RECORD: his current VA-07 platform is SILENT on marriage/LGBTQ, religious liberty, guns/2A, election integrity, CBDC, and vaccines — those are null, not inferred. Prior NJ-12 third-party aggregator (iSideWith) data is SOFT/unverified and was NOT used to score. DEVIATION FLAG: the silence on life/marriage on the CURRENT platform is notable for a Christian-conservative scorecard; prior aggregator data suggests he'd accept legal same-sex marriage with a church carve-out (unverified).

0.note: Documented pro-life: argued (on his podcast, per Ballotpedia) that abortion is not a constitutional right; iSideWith lists his stance as Pro-life. Affirms the pro-life position.
Runs the Darius Mayfield Foundation, which advocates for school choice; prior campaigns emphasized vouchers/school choice — supports school choice.
Campaign platform supports 'strong border security, including completion of the wall and enforcing immigration laws.'

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (2026 R Candidate · former NSC Director for Iraq)

profile.background: 2026 Republican candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (seat held by Rep. Eugene Vindman, D). Retired Army Lt. Col.; former NSC Director for Iraq (under Bush and Obama); foreign-policy analyst (New America, Mantid International); Wheaton College degree. Declared 9/16/2025; contested GOP primary Aug 4, 2026 (verified active, last confirmed campaigning April-May 2026).

douglas-ollivant.notes_set: 2026 Republican candidate, VA-07 (one of five in the GOP primary). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — social-media/self-statement protocol.] CANDIDACY CURRENCY VERIFIED: active, NOT withdrawn — declared 9/16/2025, still campaigning as of a 5/14/2026 Blue Virginia report; contested GOP primary AUGUST 4, 2026. Scored only from clear campaign self-statements (dougforvirginia.com/issues): 'Protecting Girls in Sports and Spaces' (biblical_marriage q2 TRUE — affirms female-only girls' sports/spaces, though in deliberately moderate language); 'restore fiscal responsibility' + 'reducing wasteful spending' (economic q2 TRUE — opposes deficit spending; no explicit balanced-budget-amendment position); a regenerative-ag / direct-farmer-to-consumer / small-farm agriculture plank (industry_capture q3 TRUE — small-farm protections). THIN RECORD: SILENT on abortion/life, religious liberty, guns/2A, election integrity, CBDC, vaccines — all null. HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION FLAG (noted, not scored from a slogan): his 'Peace Through Strength' / 'military superiority to deter adversaries' posture + establishment foreign-policy pedigree (NSC under both Bush AND Obama, New America fellow) reads as INTERVENTIONIST — likely to score AGAINST the foreign-policy-restraint rubric if/when he makes specific Ukraine/AUMF/forever-wars statements; left null pending a citable position. Moderate tone on trans/sports relative to typical GOP-primary framing.

2.note: 'Protecting Girls in Sports and Spaces' plank supports keeping girls' sports/private spaces female-only (biological-sex basis), though framed in moderate language.
Campaigns on restoring fiscal responsibility and reducing wasteful spending so Virginians keep more of what they earn — opposes deficit spending (no explicit balanced-budget-amendment statement).

3.note: Agriculture plank backs regenerative farming, direct farmer-to-consumer sales, and reducing supply-chain barriers — supports small-farm protections.
